meta.verifiedNote: Replace this file with each new PAGASA bulletin. Set latlonExact true and enter exact decimals from the PAGASA bulletin PDF to plot true PAGASA coordinates.

current.name: INDAY
current.positionRef: 1,170 km E of Northern Luzon (inside PAR)
current.pressure: Not specified in latest PAGASA bulletin (930 hPa at 5 AM)
current.outlook: Maintains super-typhoon strength until Thu 09 Jul; may weaken to a typhoon by Thu evening or early Fri morning. Nearest Extreme N. Luzon by Fri 10 Jul; exits PAR Sat 11 Jul; forecast China landfall Sun 12 Jul. Likely stays over the Philippine Sea.

rainfall.note: Rainfall = PAGASA TC rainfall (Batanes) + PAGASA 3-day Southwest Monsoon outlook. Floods and landslides are likely.

marine.galeWarning: Gale Warning No. 2 (issued 5:00 PM) — northern & eastern seaboards of Luzon, and the eastern seaboard of Visayas.

waves.where: Seaboard of Isabela; eastern seaboards of Batanes and Cagayan incl. Babuyan Islands
Seaboard of Camarines Norte; remaining seaboards of Batanes & Cagayan incl. Babuyan Is.; northern seaboards of Aurora, Camarines Sur & Eastern Samar

marine.advice: Sea travel is risky for all types/tonnage of vessels in the affected seaboards. Mariners must remain in port or seek safe harbor until winds and waves subside.

satellite.note: Satellite imagery is visual context only. Cloud position/appearance must not be used to infer storm movement — the official center and track come from PAGASA.
